summary: Iter 197 — Geofence Phase 2 (offline attendance punching) + RBAC frontend route protection review. BACKEND: 4/4 pytest passed (test_iter_geofence_phase2.py). Verified (a) /api/attendance/my-geo-policy returns offline_punch_enabled=False by default and True when firm master offline_geofence_enabled is toggled on; (b) POST /api/attendance/punch with offline=true + client_punch_at ~2h ago persists the record with `at`=IST-wall-clock of the capture time (not server now), correct `date`, offline_punch=true, synced_at set; (c) repeat POST with same client_dedupe_id returns duplicate:true, no duplicate row in db.attendance (count stays 1); (d) offline=true + client_punch_at 30 days in past falls outside sanity window → punch stored with server-now IST time. All test punches were deleted after each test; firm.offline_geofence_enabled restored to False; test employee's gps_punch_enabled restored. FRONTEND (RBAC route protection, desktop 1920x800, admin-pin-login → Password mode): SUPER-ADMIN sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com/sharma123 verified — direct URL to /salary-run, /messages, /roles all render normally with NO Access Denied (confirmed on-screen: /roles page shows 'Roles & Permissions' with 6 roles and Test HR staff user). SUB-ADMIN testsub@sksharma.co/testsub123 (scoped to City Care Hospital, perms: employees:read/write, attendance_policy:read/write, companies:read) BUG CONFIRMED: after login+firm select, page.goto('/salary-run') and page.goto('/messages') both REDIRECT the URL to /portal-dashboard instead of showing testID route-access-denied. location.pathname becomes /portal-dashboard within 500ms and never reverts. No 'Access Denied' screen, no 'Go to Dashboard' button (denied-go-home) is rendered. The RBAC gate at AdminWebShell.tsx:649-675 IS correctly excluding /salary-run and /messages from the sub-admin's nav (sidebar hides those buttons — verified in screenshot), and the routeDenied useMemo would compute true for those paths, BUT something upstream is rewriting the URL to /portal-dashboard before AdminWebShell can render the deny screen. Allowed routes /kyc-tracker and /bulk-employee-correction load normally (URL preserved, no deny screen). FRONTEND 1/2 (offline punch queue mobile flow) and FRONTEND 4 (staff HR direct /roles) NOT tested in UI due to context budget; backend proofs cover the core offline-punch contract.

ui_bugs.issue: Sub-admin direct URL navigation to nav-gated routes (/salary-run, /messages) redirects to /portal-dashboard instead of rendering the Access Denied screen. Reproduction: log in as testsub@sksharma.co/testsub123 → choose firm → page.goto('/salary-run') → location.pathname becomes /portal-dashboard and no [data-testid=route-access-denied] is present. Same behaviour for /messages. RBAC filter correctly HIDES the sidebar buttons for those routes, but the URL rewrite bypasses the routeDenied render.
'Go to Dashboard' button (testID denied-go-home) never appears for sub-admin on /salary-run because the deny screen itself never renders (see above). Cannot verify per spec.

integration_issues.issue: The premise of the feature — 'RBAC FRONTEND ROUTE PROTECTION: AdminWebShell now denies direct-URL access to nav-gated pages the user\'s permissions don\'t allow' — is NOT functioning for /salary-run and /messages. Sub-admins are silently redirected to /portal-dashboard, so they never see any indication that a page was blocked (only that their URL changed).

issues: Pre-existing noise (already flagged iter 194-196): shadow* deprecated → use boxShadow; invalid style props outline/background/padding shorthand across many screens.

action_items: PRIORITY: Investigate why direct URL /salary-run and /messages redirect sub-admin to /portal-dashboard instead of rendering the deny screen. Possible culprits: (a) an implicit index.tsx redirect happening on cold-load because AuthContext hasn't rehydrated yet when the sub-admin's rendered route is decided; (b) a routeDenied side-effect (e.g. useEffect that calls router.replace on denial instead of just rendering deny UI); (c) a firm-scope guard in salary-run/messages that redirects to dashboard when sub_admin has no access. The RBAC feature must show the Access Denied screen (testID route-access-denied) so users understand access was blocked and can use the Go to Dashboard button.
Verify /kyc-tracker + /bulk-employee-correction still show for sub-admin (confirmed working) and that /salary-run + /messages show the deny screen (currently broken).
After fix, retest FRONTEND 3 (sub-admin denied), FRONTEND 4 (staff HR /roles), FRONTEND 5 (super admin regression — already verified working).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/attendance/punch idempotency and IST-wall-clock offline conversion at server.py:8455-8570 look correct — sanity window [now-7d, now+10min] cleanly gates stale/future capture times; client_punch_at is stored verbatim for audit; date derives from the (possibly-honoured) capture time.

offlinePunch.ts uses IndexedDB for web and AsyncStorage for native — good separation. genId is time+random which is fine for dedupe keys. flushQueue drops items that fail with 'duplicate'/409 (idempotent-safe). No leaks observed.
AdminWebShell routeDenied useMemo (~lines 649-675) computes correctly but is short-circuited by an upstream redirect for at least 2 nav-gated paths — see UI bug.
geo_policy.py my_geo_policy correctly surfaces `offline_punch_enabled` from firm master doc (bool cast) — clean contract.

success_rate.frontend: RBAC sub-admin gating FAILS for /salary-run + /messages (redirects instead of deny). Super admin regression PASS. Sub-admin allowed routes PASS. Offline queue mobile flow (FRONTEND 1/2) + staff HR /roles (FRONTEND 4) NOT UI-tested this iter.

seed_data_creation: None persisted. Test attendance rows and firm/user flags were created & cleaned inside pytest fixtures. Verified post-run: firm.offline_geofence_enabled=False, user.gps_punch_enabled unset, no leftover ITERGP2_ punches.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter_geofence_phase2.py for regression on offline-punch behaviour. The 4 tests are self-contained (fixtures enable gps_punch_enabled for TEST50 & clean up ITERGP2_ dedupe rows). For frontend RBAC verification: log via /admin-pin-login → click testID mode-password → fill admin-identifier-input + admin-password-input → click admin-pin-submit. Sub-admin lands on /firm-select — click 'Open workspace' on City Care Hospital, then page.goto('/salary-run'). CURRENTLY BROKEN: URL rewrites to /portal-dashboard within 500ms; expected: [data-testid=route-access-denied] visible with denied-go-home button. Only routes that are permission-DENIED show this redirect; allowed routes (/kyc-tracker, /bulk-employee-correction) do NOT redirect. Super admin (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / sharma123) is unaffected — /salary-run, /messages, /roles all render normally.
rca_of_issue: RBAC route deny screen not shown for /salary-run + /messages. Reproduction: (1) login sub-admin testsub@sksharma.co/testsub123 on /admin-pin-login (Password mode); (2) firm-select → City Care Hospital; (3) page.goto('/salary-run'); (4) within 500ms location.pathname flips to /portal-dashboard. No route-access-denied element is ever rendered. Likely cause: an upstream `router.replace('/portal-dashboard')` or `<Redirect href='/portal-dashboard' />` fires before AdminWebShell mounts and evaluates routeDenied — perhaps the salary-run or messages screen has a firm-permission gate (like advances.tsx which does exactly this: `if (deniedByFirm) return <Redirect href='/portal-dashboard' />;` at advances.tsx:259) but salary-run.tsx/messages.tsx grep shows no such redirect, so it's more likely an index.tsx cold-load rehydration race or a global guard in the tabs layout. Mitigation: ensure the routeDenied path renders the deny screen synchronously and does not race with any post-mount router.replace. Verify with the same reproduction after fix — [data-testid=route-access-denied] must be visible and location.pathname must remain /salary-run.
